title: Antivirus & EDR 🦠
description: How modern systems hunt for malware signatures.

slides.title: Malware Hunting 🦠
slides.content:
### Antivirus (AV)

Antivirus software looks for the **signatures** (digital fingerprints) of known viruses. If it matches, it blocks it.

*   **Limit**: It can't stop a virus it has never seen before (Zero-Days).

slides.title: The Next Level: EDR 🚀
slides.content:
### Endpoint Detection & Response

**EDR** is the smart cousin of Antivirus. It doesn't just look for signatures; it looks for **behavior**.

*   If a calculator app suddenly starts trying to download files from Russia, EDR says "Hey, calculators don't do that!" and shuts it down.

quizData.question: Why is behavior-based detection (EDR) considered superior to simple signature-based detection (AV)?

options.text: It can detect brand new, unknown threats

quizData.explanation: Behavior-based detection can spot malicious patterns even if the specific file signature hasn't been seen before.
